title: Sunrun Q1 2026 Financial Results Summary
text: Sunrun reported strong operational metrics for first quarter of 2026 achieving Aggregate Subscriber Value of $1.1 billion in Q1 while maintaining focus on profitability. The storage attachment rate reached record 73%, up from 69% prior year with company installing over 251,000 storage and solar systems representing approximately 4.3 gigawatt hours of networked storage capacity. Net income attributable to common stockholders reached $167.6 million or $0.71 per basic share, though company experienced negative cash generation of $59 million during quarter, primarily due to timing shifts in project finance activities. The company added 17,665 subscribers in Q1, representing 25% decline compared to prior year with total subscribers growing to 1,014,945 as of March 31, 2026, up 11% year-over-year. Management paid down $92 million in recourse debt during quarter and extended warehouse facility to 2029-2030, increasing commitments to $2.7 billion. The company maintained full-year 2026 guidance for cash generation between $250 million and $450 million, excluding equipment safe harbor investments. Upfront Net Subscriber Value reached $5,136, up over $4,000 per subscriber compared to prior year driven by larger system sizes, higher storage attachment rate, higher average ITC level, and lower capital costs.
